title: nasturtium amphibium
intro: Nasturtium amphibium, also known as watercress or Indian cress, is a semi-aquatic plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The plant's leaves and stems have been traditionally used to treat conditions such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, and have also been found to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Nasturtium amphibium can be consumed as a raw addition to salads or smoothies, or prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, although its preparation and dosage should be guided by a healthcare professional.
